summary: This brief technical post shares a tip from Scott Hanselman about deploying multiple /bin folders in ASP.NET applications. Jeff Atwood confirms he tested the technique and it works, demonstrating how to add a pre-compiled utility to an existing website by placing it in a subfolder with its own binaries. The key is modifying the parent app's Web.config to set up the probing path for child assemblies, plus a small modification to the child .aspx page header. This approach enables a pseudo plugin architecture for ASP.NET websites.
key_points: Multiple /bin folders can be deployed in ASP.NET by configuring assembly probing paths in Web.config
This technique enables a pseudo plugin architecture for existing ASP.NET websites
Pre-compiled utilities can be dropped into subfolders with their own binaries
The child .aspx page header requires an Assembly directive modification that can be made permanent in the source
money_quotes: What's really cool about this is that it lets you build a pseudo plugin architecture into your existing ASP.NET website.
Scott documents it perfectly; I'm here to tell you that I tried it, and it works.
If you don't do this, you'll get 'Assembly not found' exceptions.
key_insight: ASP.NET's assembly probing path configuration allows you to create a simple plugin architecture by deploying pre-compiled components in subfolders with their own /bin directories.
